title: Nidularium citrinum
taxonomy: Nidularium citrinum is a species of flowering plant in the family Bromeliaceae. This classification places it within the bromeliad family, which includes pineapples and air plants. Nidularium citrinum is native to the Americas, particularly in Brazil, where it grows in tropical and subtropical regions. The plant's classification as a member of the Bromeliaceae family is due to its unique characteristics, such as its rosette shape and waxy leaves. As a member of this family, Nidularium citrinum shares characteristics with other bromeliads, including its ability to store water in its leaves.
